original: First pizza party in space held by astronauts of the International Space Station
Pioneer 10 passes Jupiter (1st fly-by of an outer planet) [Dec 4-GMT]
First human heart transplant performed in South Africa by Dr.Christiaan Barnardon Louis Washkansky
Richard Pearse, New Zealand inventor and aviator who designed and flew one of the world's first airplanes, born at Waitohi Flat, New Zealand (d. 1953)
Andrew Stanton, American Academy Award-winning animator and film director (Wall-E; Finding Nemo), born in Rockport, Massachusetts
NASA loses radio contact with the Mars Polar Lander moments before the spacecraft enters the Martian atmosphere
David Attenboroughwarns collapse of civilisation and the natural world on the horizon at UN climate summit in Poland
7-year old Ryan of Ryan Toysreview is the year's highest-paid YouTube star earning $22 million
"Do They Know It's Christmas" single written byBob Geldofand Midge Ure and sung by an all-star cast under the banner of Band Aid is released in the UK
Soviet PresidentMikhail Gorbachevand US PresidentGeorge H. W. Bush, declare the Cold War over
First recorded successful separation of conjoined twins Elisabet and Catherina Meijerin, completed by Swiss surgeon Johannes Fatio in Basel
John Backus, American computer scientist and inventor (FORTRAN computer language), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (d. 2007)
Peter Schultz, American scientist and co-inventor of optical fiber, born in Brooklyn, New York
Sir Thomas Herriot introduces potatoes to England from Colombia
Neon lights first publicly displayed (Paris Auto Show)
Detective novelistAgatha Christiemysteriously disappears for 11 days
Willis Carrierpresents his influential "Rational Psychrometric Formulae", on air conditioning to the American Society of Mechanical Engineers - makes air conditioning possible
Frederick Douglasspublishes first issue of his newspaper "North Star"
Oberlin College in Ohio, the first truly coeducational college, opens
Elvis Presley's Comeback Special airs on NBC, his 1st live performance in seven years, re-launching his singing career
Jake T. Austin, American actor (Wizards of Waverley Place), born in New York City
Marques Brownlee, American Youtuber (MKBHD) and ultimate frisbee player, born in Maplewood, New Jersey
Basketball iconWilt Chamberlainmakes his much anticipated varsity debut; scores 52 points and grabs 31 rebounds, breaking both all-time Kansas records in an 87–69 win against Northwestern
Katarina Witt, German figure skater (Olympic gold ladies singles 1984, 88; World C'ship gold 1984-85, 87-88), born in Staaken, Germany
Bucky Lasek, American skateboarder (10 x Summer X Games gold), born in Baltimore, Maryland
Alicia Sacramone, American gymnast (World C'ship gold 2005, 07, 10, 11), born in Boston, Massachusetts
AstronomerAnders Celsiustakes measurements that confirm Newton's theory that the earth was an ellipsoid rather than the previously accepted sphere

English Assyriologist George Smith reads his translation of The Great Flood from the "Epic of Gilgamesh" to the Society of Biblical Archaeology, with striking similarities to the later flood of Noah in "Genesis"
Irishman James Kelly (24) and British soldier Jack Smith (34) fight longest bareknuckle boxing bout - 6 hours and 15 minutes, at Fiery Creek, Victoria, Australia
"Rubber Soul", the sixth studio album by the Beatles, is released in the United Kingdom
The Beatles begin final UK concert tour in Glasgow, Scotland
Samuel Crompton, English inventor (mule-jenny spinning machine), born in 10 Firwood Fold, Bolton, Lancashire, England (d. 1827)
After nearly 20 years of planning and construction, the Quebec Bridge finally opens to traffic after two prior collapses

MLB Rules Committee adopts a series of changes designed to increase amount of offensive run production; decreases strike zone size and lowers height of pitcher's mound from 15" to 10"; changes are successful
Igor Larionov, Russian Hockey Hall of Fame center (Stanley Cup 1997-98, 2002, Detroit Red Wings; Olympic gold 1984, 88), born in Moscow, Russia
Mike Ramsey, American ice hockey defenceman (Olympic gold 1980; NHL All-Star 1982-83, 85-86), born in Minneapolis, Minnesota
Laura Schuler, Canadian ice hockey forward and coach (World C'ship gold 1990, 92, 97; coach Dartmouth College), born in Scarborough, Ontario
Eva Němcová, Czech basketball forward (WNBA record 66 consecutive free throws 2000; Cleveland Rockers), born in Prague, Czech Republic
László Cseh, Hungarian swimmer (World C'ship gold 400m medley 2005, 200m butterfly 2015; 7 x Olympic medals), born in Budapest, Hungary
Christian Benteke, Belgian soccer striker (93 caps; Genk, Crystal Palace, Liverpool, Aston Villa), born in Kinshasa, Zaire
David Villa, Spanish soccer striker (98 caps; Valencia, Barcelona), born in Langreo, Spain
Vernon White, American mixed martial arts fighter (King of the Cage Middleweight Champion; UFC), born in Sparks, Nevada
Anna Chlumsky, American actress (My Girl, Veep), born in Chicago, Illinois
Tiffany Haddish, American comedian and actress (Girls Trip), born in Los Angeles, California
Angèle [Van Laeken], Belgian singer-songwriter (Brol; Nonante-Cinq), born in Uccle, Belgium
Sherri DuPree-Bemis, American pop-rock singer-songwriter (Eisley), born in Tyler, Texas
Joseph McManners, English actor (The Little Prince) and pop-classical crossover singer ("In Dreams", "Bright Eyes") , born in Canterbury, England
Montell Jordan [Barnett], American R&B singer, record producer ("This Is How We Do It"), and pastor, born in Los Angeles, California
Trina [Katrina Laverne Taylor], American rapper (Glamorest Life), born in Miami, Florida
Lindsey Hunter, American basketball guard (NBA C'ship 2002, 04; LA Lakers, Detroit Pistons), born in Utica, Mississippi
Mark Boucher, South African cricket wicketkeeper (147 Tests, record 555 dismissals; 295 ODIs), born in East London, Eastern Cape, South Africa
Adal Ramones, Mexican comedian and television host, born in Monterrey, Mexico
Rainbow Sun Francks, Canadian actor (Stargate Atlantis), born in Toronto, Ontario
Amy Helm, American singer-songwriter (Ollabelle), born in Woodstock, New York
First official US flag raised (Grand Union Flag) aboard naval vessel USS Alfred
NY Lotto pays $45 million to twelve winner (#s are 1-8-13-18-28-48)
XCOR Aerospace makes first manned rocket aircraft delivery of US Mail in Mojave, California.
US army decides it will not allow an oil pipeline to be built in North Dakota, after months of protests by The Standing Rock Sioux Tribe
Edith Sampson sworn-in as 1st Black female judge in the state of Illinois
Gilbert Stuart, American portrait painter (unfinished portrait of George Washington), born in Saunderstown, Rhode Island (d. 1828)
